publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-06-21 18:44:47 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: When Rosen retired from his day job in April 2000, he selected ten images from each of his four books to comprise "Michael Rosen's Top 40." In 2003, he completed three sets of high quality 16x20 silver prints of these images. This is one set. The others are with The Kinsey Institute and Gayle Rubin. Along with the prints, the collection includes Rosen's description of each print and signed copies of books with his art.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Photographer of erotic, sexual, and radical sexual subjects since 1977. The Seattle Erotic Art Festival named Rosen a Master of Erotic Art in 2012, and the Society of Janus inducted him into its Hall of Fame in 2014. Four books of his images have been published: Sexual Magic: the S/M Photographs in 1986, Sexual Portraits: Photographs of Radical Sexuality in 1990, Sexual Art: Photographs That Test the Limits in 1994, and Lust & Romance: Rated X Fine Art Photographs in 1998. His images also appeared in Femalia (1993, Down There Press), I Am My Lover (1997, Down There Press, second edition), Vanilla Sex (2007), Slow Dance (2016), and Jump (2017).

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: When Rosen retired from his day job in April 2000, he selected ten images from each of his four books to comprise "Michael Rosen's Top 40." In 2003, he completed three sets of high quality 16x20 silver prints of these images. This is one set. The others are with The Kinsey Institute and Gayle Rubin. Along with the prints, the collection includes Rosen's description of each print and signed copies of books with his art.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Related material in: Joani Blank papers, #7827 and Honey Lee Cottrell papers, #7822.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Michael A. Rosen's Top 40 work, #7847. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
